common.brand.name: MasterClass
common.locale.label: Language
common.footer.label: Legal
home.title: Welcome back.
home.lead: Pick up where you left off, or start a new class with your AI tutor.
auth.title: Sign in
auth.body: Sign-in + account recovery land in the auth feature. Lands in the B-children tickets of SEV-4.
auth.form.legal: By continuing, you agree to our Terms and Privacy Policy.
auth.login.title: Welcome back.
auth.login.sub: Sign in to continue where you left off.
auth.signup.title: Start your first lesson.
auth.signup.sub: A private, calm place to speak more English.
auth.field.email.label: EMAIL
auth.field.name.label: YOUR NAME
auth.field.password.label: PASSWORD
auth.switch.to_login: Already have an account? Log in.
auth.error.signupConflict: That email already has an account — try logging in.
auth.error.network: We couldn't reach the server. Check your connection.
auth.error.unknown: Something went wrong. Please try again.
onboarding.title: Onboarding
onboarding.body: Welcome flow + CEFR placement land in onboarding. Lands in the B-children tickets of SEV-4.
onboarding.step1.title: Let's set your language.
onboarding.step1.sub: Pick the language we'll use to explain things — you'll learn English either way.
onboarding.step2.title: Meet your teacher.
onboarding.step2.sub: You can change this anytime from your profile.
onboarding.step2.teacher.ana.name: Ana
onboarding.step2.teacher.ana.intro: Hi, I'm Ana. We'll start slow, and I'll follow your pace.
onboarding.step2.teacher.ana.portrait_al: Portrait of Ana, a warm English tutor.
onboarding.step2.teacher.daniel.name: Daniel
onboarding.step2.teacher.daniel.intro: Hey, I'm Daniel. We'll talk a lot — you'll do most of the talking.
onboarding.step2.teacher.daniel.portrait: Portrait of Daniel, a warm English tutor.
onboarding.step3.title: One more thing before we start.
onboarding.step3.sub: How do you like being corrected? {teacher} will match your choice.
onboarding.step3.tone.warm.label: Warm
onboarding.step3.tone.warm.preview: "You're doing fine — let's just polish this one word together."
onboarding.step3.tone.direct.label: Direct
onboarding.step3.tone.direct.preview: "Small fix: it's 'have been', not 'have being'. Try it again."
onboarding.step3.tone.relaxed.label: Relaxed
onboarding.step3.tone.relaxed.preview: "No rush. Take the sentence again whenever you're ready."
classroom.title: Classroom
classroom.body: Voice + text tutor classroom lands here. Lands in the B-children tickets of SEV-4.
classroom.board.earlier_notes: {count, plural, one {# earlier note} other {# earlier notes}}
classroom.mic.permission_hint: Click Allow to let me hear you
classroom.mic.error_toast: We couldn't reach the mic — check permissions.
classroom.transcript.system.session_resu: Welcome back — we're continuing where you left off
classroom.transcript.system.session_end: Great work — see you next time.
classroom.gallery.title: Avatar & mic states
classroom.gallery.avatar.heading: Avatar states
classroom.gallery.mic.heading: Mic states
classroom.demo.lesson.title: Past simple — irregular verbs
classroom.demo.lesson.objective: practice past simple with irregular verbs
classroom.demo.teacher.greeting: Welcome back. Let's warm up with a few sentences about yesterday.
classroom.demo.teacher.nudge: Tell me one thing you did yesterday — in full sentences.
classroom.demo.vocabulary.translation: a happy accident or pleasant surprise
classroom.demo.vocabulary.example: Finding that café was pure serendipity.
classroom.demo.grammar.rule: Past simple: use base form + -ed for regular verbs, irregular verbs change form (go → went).
classroom.demo.grammar.example: I went to the park yesterday.
classroom.demo.exercise.prompt: Complete: Yesterday I ___ to the market. (go)
materials.title: Materials
materials.lead: Lessons, vocabulary decks, exercises, and session summaries generated by your AI tutor — filter by level or topic.
materials.empty.lesson: No lessons match yet. Try relaxing the filters or generate a new one.
materials.empty.vocabulary: No vocabulary decks match yet. Generate one to get started.
materials.empty.exercise: No exercises match yet. Generate one to practice.
materials.empty.summary: No summaries match yet. They appear here after each class.
materials.submeta: {count, plural, one {# lesson} other {# lessons}} · {inProgress, plural, one {# in progress} other {# in progress}}
materials.filter.scopeNote: Filters apply to all lessons only.
materials.empty.inProgress: Nothing here yet. Finish a lesson to see it here.
materials.empty.saved: No saved lessons yet. Tap the bookmark on any lesson to save it.
materials.empty.all: Your library will show up here once lessons are available.
review.vocab.intro: {new, plural, one {# new word} other {# new words}} · {reviewed, plural, one {# reviewed} other {# reviewed}}
review.corrections.empty: No corrections this lesson. You spoke carefully.
review.title: Review
review.lead: Short, spaced review of what you've practiced — just the items your tutor thinks need another pass today.
review.body: Spaced-repetition review of past material lands here. Lands in the B-children tickets of SEV-4.
review.empty: Nothing to review right now. Come back after your next class or generate new material.
review.grades.aria: How well did you recall it?
review.done.title: Nice — session complete.
progress.hero.returning.1: Welcome back — your English is still in there.
progress.hero.returning.2: Nice to see you again. Let's pick up the thread.
progress.hero.returning.3: A pause isn't a loss. Ready when you are.
progress.hero.returning.4: Your voice is waiting — let's warm it up.
progress.hero.levelUp.1: You stepped up a level. Feel the new room.
progress.hero.levelUp.2: New bucket, new ceilings. Let's explore them.
progress.hero.levelUp.3: Your English just got larger. Say more.
progress.hero.levelUp.4: This is what growth sounds like.
progress.hero.consistent.1: You're finding your voice in English.
progress.hero.consistent.2: Steady practice is quietly reshaping you.
progress.hero.consistent.3: Today's lesson meets a stronger student.
progress.hero.consistent.4: Fluency is built in weeks like this one.
progress.hero.consistent.5: Showing up is most of the work.
progress.hero.early.1: You're at the beginning of something good.
progress.hero.early.2: First sessions set the tone. Let's set it kindly.
progress.hero.early.3: Your accent is yours — we'll build on it.
progress.hero.early.4: Welcome. Your English journey is underway.
progress.hero.default.1: Your English, one honest sentence at a time.
progress.hero.default.4: Let's listen, speak, and get a little braver.
progress.streak.label: {count, plural, one {day streak} other {day streak}}
progress.streak.dayAria: {active, select, yes {Studied on {date}} other {No session on {date}}}
progress.level.nextHint: About {sessionsEstimate, plural, one {# session} other {# sessions}} until {nextBucket}.
progress.level.atCap: You're at the top tier — keep polishing.
progress.sessions.empty: You haven't finished a lesson yet. Start one from Materials.
progress.skills.empty: Start a lesson to see your skill mix.
history.title: Lesson history
history.lead: Revisit past sessions — summaries, transcripts, and the corrections your AI tutor made along the way.
history.list.empty: No sessions match yet. Try clearing filters or completing a new lesson.
history.detail.empty: Select a session from the list to see its transcript and corrections.
history.detail.pronunciation.empty: No pronunciation changes tracked this session.
history.corrections.heading: Corrections
history.corrections.empty: No corrections this session — nice work.
profile.title: Your space
profile.subtitle: A few things you can change without leaving the lesson flow.
profile.account.title: Account
profile.account.nameHelp: Shown on your dashboard and lesson reviews.
profile.account.nameRequired: Add a name so your teacher can greet you.
profile.account.emailReadonly: To change your email, contact support.
profile.password.currentWrong: That current password doesn't match what we have.
profile.teacher.title: Teacher
profile.prefs.title: Preferences
profile.prefs.langDesc: Used across the app. Separate from teaching language.
profile.prefs.densityDesc: Row spacing in your Materials list. You can still change it from the Materials toolbar.
profile.prefs.toneDesc: How your teacher gives feedback. You can change this anytime.
profile.danger.title: Leaving or ending things
profile.danger.signOutHelp: You'll be asked for your password next time you return.
profile.danger.deleteHelp: Removes your profile, lessons, and progress. This can't be undone.
profile.danger.modalWhat: This will permanently remove your lessons, progress, and preferences.
profile.danger.modalWhen: We'll sign you out right after. You can always sign up again with the same email later.
profile.danger.modalError: We couldn't delete the account right now — please try again.
profile.toast.teacherSaved: {teacher} will be your teacher from the next lesson.
profile.toast.teacherError: We couldn't save that change — try again.
profile.toast.revertError: We couldn't save that — {field} is back to what it was.
assessment.title: Let's find your level.
assessment.lead: A short adaptive check, powered by your AI teacher. Answer by voice or text — we'll tune the next question to how you're doing.
assessment.avatar.label: Your AI teacher
assessment.status.completed: Nice work — here's where you stand.
assessment.input.label: Your response
assessment.textarea.label: Type your answer
assessment.transcript.label: Your responses so far
assessment.result.label: Assessment result
assessment.result.title: Estimated CEFR level
assessment.q.a1.read: Read this sign aloud: 'Exit'. What does it mean?
assessment.q.a1.write: Write one sentence about your morning.
assessment.q.a1.listen: Say 'hello' in English and one more greeting.
assessment.q.a1.speak: Introduce yourself in two short sentences.
assessment.q.a2.read: Describe a photo of a park in three sentences.
assessment.q.a2.write: Write a short message inviting a friend for coffee.
assessment.q.a2.listen: Explain what you did yesterday in a few sentences.
assessment.q.a2.speak: Tell me what you usually eat for breakfast.
assessment.q.b1.read: Summarize a short news story about the weather.
assessment.q.b1.write: Write a paragraph about a recent trip.
assessment.q.b1.listen: Give three tips for learning a new language.
assessment.q.b1.speak: Describe your job or studies and what you enjoy about it.
assessment.q.b2.read: Give your opinion on remote work vs. office work.
assessment.q.b2.write: Explain a process you know well in a few steps.
assessment.q.b2.listen: Retell a short podcast segment in your own words.
assessment.q.b2.speak: Argue for or against letting children use smartphones.
assessment.q.c1.read: Analyze the pros and cons of electric vehicles.
assessment.q.c1.write: Write a concise email resolving a customer complaint.
assessment.q.c1.listen: Critique an idea you've recently read or heard about.
assessment.q.c1.speak: Describe a complex problem at work and how you'd solve it.
assessment.q.c2.read: Interpret a metaphor from a piece of literature you know.
assessment.q.c2.write: Draft a nuanced argument about artificial intelligence.
assessment.q.c2.listen: Explain the subtleties of a cultural idiom in English.
assessment.q.c2.speak: Debate whether universal basic income is a good policy.
lesson.preview.title: Your adaptive lesson.
lesson.preview.lead: Generated from your placement result — communicative, paced, and tuned to your weakest skill.
lesson.preview.empty: Finish your placement to see a lesson plan here.
lesson.activity.objective.warmup: Break the ice and get comfortable speaking.
lesson.activity.objective.grammar: Use a grammar pattern on purpose.
lesson.activity.objective.speaking: Say it out loud, even imperfectly.
lesson.activity.objective.reading: Read for meaning, not every word.
lesson.activity.objective.correction: Fix one thing the teacher flagged.
lesson.activity.objective.review: Revisit what stuck — and what didn't.
lesson.activity.objective.cooldown: Recap the lesson in your own words.
lesson.adjuster.reason.up: Stepping up — you're in the pocket.
lesson.correction.feedback.close: Close — a couple of words to sharpen.
lesson.correction.feedback.try_again: Let's try again, a little slower.
lesson.correction.feedback.unknown: I didn't catch that — say it once more.
lesson.correction.hint.try_slower: Slow the word down and keep the stress.
lesson.curriculum.a1.introductions.gramm: Use the verb 'to be' to introduce yourself.
lesson.curriculum.a1.introductions.liste: Listen to three people introduce themselves — who is who?
lesson.curriculum.a1.introductions.readi: Read three short intros and pick the one that matches the photo.
lesson.curriculum.a1.introductions.writi: Write a two-line introduction about yourself.
lesson.curriculum.a1.daily_routine.vocab: Wake up, have breakfast, work, sleep.
lesson.curriculum.a1.daily_routine.liste: Listen to Maya's morning and put the steps in order.
lesson.curriculum.a1.daily_routine.readi: Read a short schedule and answer two questions.
lesson.curriculum.a1.daily_routine.writi: Write three sentences about your weekday.
lesson.curriculum.a2.past_events.grammar: Past simple — regular and common irregulars.
lesson.curriculum.a2.past_events.vocabul: Yesterday, last weekend, ago, in the morning.
lesson.curriculum.a2.past_events.listeni: Listen to someone describe last weekend — what did they do?
lesson.curriculum.a2.past_events.speakin: Tell me about something you did last weekend.
lesson.curriculum.a2.past_events.reading: Read a short blog entry about a past event and answer questions.
lesson.curriculum.a2.past_events.writing: Write four sentences about something memorable you did.
lesson.curriculum.a2.travel.listening: Listen to a travel announcement and note the gate and time.
lesson.curriculum.a2.travel.speaking: Ask for directions to the nearest station.
lesson.curriculum.a2.travel.reading: Read a short itinerary and answer two questions.
lesson.curriculum.a2.travel.writing: Write a short message to a friend about a trip.
lesson.curriculum.b1.opinions.grammar: Modal verbs for opinion and possibility.
lesson.curriculum.b1.opinions.vocabulary: Agree, disagree, in my view, on the other hand.
lesson.curriculum.b1.opinions.listening: Listen to two people disagree politely — what's the main point?
lesson.curriculum.b1.opinions.speaking: Say whether you agree with a short statement and explain why.
lesson.curriculum.b1.opinions.reading: Read a short opinion column and identify the thesis.
lesson.curriculum.b1.opinions.writing: Write a short paragraph taking a side on a small issue.
lesson.curriculum.b1.work.grammar: Present perfect vs. past simple at work.
lesson.curriculum.b1.work.listening: Listen to a team stand-up and summarize the blockers.
lesson.curriculum.b1.work.speaking: Describe a recent task at work or school.
lesson.curriculum.b1.work.reading: Read a short email thread and identify the decision.
lesson.curriculum.b1.work.writing: Write a short status update for a team lead.
lesson.curriculum.b2.media.listening: Listen to a short news clip and report what the speaker said.
lesson.curriculum.b2.media.speaking: Summarize an article you read this week in under a minute.
lesson.curriculum.b2.media.reading: Read a news summary and identify sources and claims.
lesson.curriculum.b2.media.writing: Write a neutral summary of a short article.
lesson.curriculum.b2.environment.listeni: Listen to a podcast excerpt and note two concrete actions mentioned.
lesson.curriculum.b2.environment.speakin: Describe one thing you do to reduce your footprint.
lesson.curriculum.b2.environment.reading: Read a short policy brief and identify its main claim.
lesson.curriculum.b2.environment.writing: Write a short paragraph proposing one local policy.
lesson.curriculum.c1.abstract.listening: Listen to an abstract lecture excerpt and identify the core claim.
lesson.curriculum.c1.abstract.speaking: Explain an abstract idea you care about in one minute.
lesson.curriculum.c1.abstract.reading: Read an abstract passage and identify its underlying assumption.
lesson.curriculum.c1.abstract.writing: Write a short analytical paragraph with a clear thesis.
lesson.curriculum.c1.debate.listening: Listen to a debate clip and identify each side's strongest point.
lesson.curriculum.c1.debate.speaking: Defend a position and concede one counterpoint.
lesson.curriculum.c1.debate.reading: Read a short op-ed and identify one weak premise.
lesson.curriculum.c1.debate.writing: Write a short rebuttal to an argument you disagree with.
lesson.curriculum.c2.nuance.listening: Listen to an interview and identify a shift in register.
lesson.curriculum.c2.nuance.speaking: Explain the nuance between two near-synonyms.
lesson.curriculum.c2.nuance.reading: Read a literary excerpt and identify the subtext.
lesson.curriculum.c2.nuance.writing: Rewrite a blunt sentence twice, shifting register each time.
